{{Quote|Crush anything that stands in our way, in the name of the Dark Lord of the Sith!|Shar Dakhan|<ref name="encounter">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 3]]</ref>}}
'''Shar Dakhan''' was a [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] active during the years leading up to the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]]. Dakhan ruled over the [[Sith Worlds/Legends|Sith Space]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Ch'hodos]], which served as his primary base of operations. In the year [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], Dakhan pledged his [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] to [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]], who at the time was preparing for a war against the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. To celebrate, Dakhan staged a large war rally on Ch'hodos, replete with vast legions of [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]], warbeasts, and [[Warship/Legends|warships]]. Dakhan's fleet later served in the Sith invasion force in the Great Hyperspace War, with Dakhan himself personally commanding the Sith forces in the [[Battle of Coruscant (Great Hyperspace War)|attack]] on the Republic [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Dakhan's forces were opposed by a numerically inferior group of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and Republic soldiers, but the Sith were defeated. After the defeat in Republic space, Shar Dakhan returned to the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]] with the remaining Sith forces, and participated in a [[Second battle of Korriban|skirmish]] near [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]] against pursuing Republic forces. Towards the end of the battle, Sadow found himself trapped and fled; Dakhan subsequently took charge of the force, although he would be defeated again.

==Biography==
{{Quote|Naga Sadow commands that we capture the heart of the government!|Shar Dakhan|<ref name="encounter" />}}
Shar Dakhan, a [[Gender/Legends|male]] member of the [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]] [[Species/Legends|species]], was a [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] in the years leading up to the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]]. Dakhan ruled over the [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Ch'hodos]],<ref name="collision" /> located in the northern part of the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] area of [[Sith Worlds/Legends|Sith Space]],<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> which served as his central base.<ref name="collision" /> In [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], after the [[Death/Legends|death]] of [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Marka Ragnos]], the expansionist Sith Lord [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]] came to power. After defeating his rival, [[Ludo Kressh]], in the [[Battle of Khar Delba]], Sadow announced his plans to attack the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].<ref name="flight">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 5|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 5]]</ref> Dakhan supported Sadow and pledged his personal [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] to Sadow's war effort. Soon afterward, Dakhan hosted a war rally at his personal [[Fortress/Legends|fortress]] on Ch'hodos, replete with [[Legion/Legends|legions]] of [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] warriors, dozens of [[Warship/Legends|warships]], and [[Sith war behemoth]]s. Dakhan himself rode astride a large green [[Sith war rhino]].<ref name="collision" />

Dakhan later accompanied Sadow's invasion force in the Great Hyperspace War, and personally [[Commander/Legends|commanded]] the Sith [[Battle of Coruscant (Great Hyperspace War)|attack]] on the Republic [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] after Sadow demanded that Dakhan and the Sith force capture the planet. Dakhan's assault pushed the Republic forces back to the steps of the [[Senate Hall]].<ref name="encounter" /> Sadow aided Dakhan by enhancing his numbers with some of his Sith [[Mind trick/Legends#Force illusion|illusions]], which made it appear that his force was much larger. A small group of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and Republic soldiers defended the Senate Hall, holding the building until Sadow's concentration was broken and the illusions disappeared. Emboldened, the Jedi charged on Dakhan's force and defeated them.<ref name="dogs">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 4|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 4]]</ref> Dakhan survived the battle, and returned to the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]] with the remaining Sith forces. The Republic pursued them, and Dakhan participated in the subsequent [[second battle of Korriban]]. Sadow was forced to flee the battle towards the end, and Dakhan took over as acting Dark Lord.

During the subsequent Jedi-Republic [[Post–Great Hyperspace War counterinvasion|counterinvasion]] of the Sith Empire that followed the war's conclusion,<ref name="TEA" /><ref name="TEGtW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref><ref name="SWTORE">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia]]''</ref> acting Dark Lord Shar Dakhan<ref name="TEA" /> oversaw the defense of the Empire alongside those Sith survivors who chose to continue opposing the Republic.<ref name="GH30">{{TORcite|type=codex|Galactic History 30: Rise of the Sith Emperor}}</ref> Suicide attacks undertaken at Dakhan's orders managed to successfully expel Republic occupation forces from the [[Horuset system]],<ref name="TEA" /><ref name="GH30" /> during which time Sith survivors began crawling out of the shattered tombs and cities in which they had taken refuge.<ref name="GH30" /> In the brief lull that followed,<ref name="GH30" /> a reclusive Sith Lord named [[Tenebrae|Vitiate]] appeared in their midst and gathered the remaining eight thousand Sith Lords<ref name="GH31">{{TORcite|type=codex|Galactic History 31: The Ritual of Nathema}}</ref> in preparation for a [[Sith Emperor's ritual|ritual]] of [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith magic]] that Vitiate claimed would bring about the destruction of the Republic.<ref name="TOR:Revan">''[[The Old Republic: Revan]]''</ref>

==Personality and traits==
Shar Dakhan was an ardent supporter of Naga Sadow, going so far as to pledge his entire fleet to Sadow's war effort. Dakhan had a flair for celebration, staging a large and ornate war rally on Ch'hodos. As a symbol of his power, Dakhan wore a large gold-colored helm, adorned with horns.<ref name="collision" />

==Behind the scenes==
The character of Shar Dakhan first appeared in [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire|''Tales of the Jedi: The Fall of the Sith Empire'']], a story arc of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, written by [[Kevin J. Anderson]]. He appeared briefly in one panel of the arc's second issue, [[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 2|''Forces in Collision'']]. He later appeared in the series' third issue, in which he was given a larger role. In this appearance Dakhan was illustrated by [[Dario Carrasco, Jr.]]<ref name="collision" /> In [[2008]], Dakhan was given an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.<ref name="cswe">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> Dakhan is also mentioned in [[Paul S. Kemp]]'s novel ''[[Crosscurrent]]''. In it, his last name is misspelled as "Dakhon,"<ref name="crosscurrent">''[[Crosscurrent]]''</ref> a misspelling that also appears in [[2012]]'s ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''.
